title: AD8232 Single-Lead ECG Analog Front End
content: The AD8232 is a single-supply instrumentation amplifier optimised for biopotential (ECG) signal acquisition. It amplifies the differential electrode voltage (~1 mV peak), applies a 2-pole high-pass filter to remove motion artefacts (0.5 Hz corner), a low-pass filter at ~40 Hz, and outputs a 0–3.3 V analogue signal suitable for MCU ADC sampling.
extended_content: ECG electrodes produce a differential signal of 0.5–5 mV on a large common-mode DC offset (up to ±300 mV). The AD8232 rejects this common-mode with a CMRR > 80 dB, amplifies the differential signal by a gain of ~100 (adjustable with Rg), and drives an analogue output around a mid-supply bias (VCC/2). Two external capacitors set the high-pass corner (C_HP = 1 / (2π × R × f_HP)); typical 0.1 µF with 3.3 MΩ gives 0.48 Hz. The integrated right-leg-drive (RLD) output reduces 50/60 Hz mains interference. Leads-off detection: SDN pin pulled HIGH when any electrode is disconnected (LO+ and LO- output pins). Supply 2.0–3.5 V; current 170 µA; suited for battery-powered wearables. Sampling at ≥ 250 SPS (Arduino ADC 9.6 kSPS in practice) captures all clinical ECG bands. Pan-Tompkins algorithm can locate R-peaks and compute heart rate from the sampled waveform. Safety: AD8232 is NOT rated for clinical (IEC 60601-1) patient contact without additional isolation — it is suitable for hobbyist/research use only.
units: HR: bpm, RR: s, Rg: Ω
source: Analog Devices AD8232 Datasheet Rev B; Pan-Tompkins Algorithm (1985 IEEE Trans. Biomed. Eng.)
